description: CLI interface for TwelveLabs video AI API

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Set up TwelveLabs API credentials. Pass the key directly with --api-key/-k, or run without flags to be prompted. Use --force/-F to clear existing credentials and re-authenticate.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Remove stored TwelveLabs API credentials from the local machine.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Verify TwelveLabs API credentials are configured and valid by making a test API call. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: Run `twelvelabs auth refresh --help` for exact arguments and options.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `twelvelabs auth test --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Run `twelvelabs auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Run `twelvelabs auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `twelvelabs auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Run `twelvelabs auth profiles set-default --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `twelvelabs auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

profiles.usage_instructions: Run `twelvelabs auth profiles --help` for exact arguments and options.

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management for the TwelveLabs API. Use login to configure the API key, status to verify connectivity, and logout to clear stored credentials.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of indexes to return

list.usage_instructions: List all video indexes. Use --table/-t for formatted output, --filter/-f to search (e.g., "index_name:contains:course"), --properties/-p to select fields (id, index_name, video_count), --limit/-l to cap results (default 100).

get.help: Get details for a specific index.
get.usage_instructions: Retrieve details for a single index by its INDEX_ID (positional argument). Use --properties/-p to select specific fields.

create.usage_instructions: Create a new video index. NAME is a required positional argument. Use --engine/-e to specify the engine (default: pegasus1.2).

delete.help: Delete an index and all its videos.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete an index and all its videos. INDEX_ID is a required positional argument. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation. This is destructive and irreversible.

indexes.usage_instructions: Manage video indexes -- containers that hold videos for AI analysis. Create an index first, then upload videos to it. Use list to see all indexes, get for details on one.

arguments.name: index_id
arguments.help: The index ID to list videos from

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of videos to return

list.usage_instructions: List videos in a specific index. INDEX_ID is a required positional argument. Use --table/-t for formatted output, --filter/-f to search, --properties/-p to select fields, --limit/-l to cap results (default 100).

get.help: Get details for a specific video.
get.usage_instructions: Retrieve details for a single video. Both INDEX_ID and VIDEO_ID are required positional arguments. Use --properties/-p to select specific fields.

upload.help: Upload a video to an index. By default, waits for the video to be indexed before returning. Use --no-wait to return immediately after upload starts.

arguments.name: index_id
arguments.help: The index ID to upload to

options.name: --wait
options.help: --no-wait                  Wait for indexing to complete (default: wait)

options.name: --skip-duplicate
options.help: --force-upload             Skip if video with same filename exists (default: skip)

upload.usage_instructions: Upload a video file to an index for AI analysis. INDEX_ID and VIDEO_PATH are required positional arguments. By default, waits for indexing to complete (up to 600s). Use --no-wait to return immediately. Use --force-upload to upload even if a video with the same filename exists (default: skip duplicates).

delete.help: Delete a video from an index.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a video from an index. Both INDEX_ID and VIDEO_ID are required positional arguments. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

videos.usage_instructions: Manage videos within indexes. Upload videos for AI analysis, list videos in an index, get details, or delete. INDEX_ID is always the first positional argument.

text.help: Generate text from an indexed video using a custom prompt. Provide either --prompt or --prompt-file, not both. Output is sent to stdout for easy piping.

options.name: --prompt-file
options.help: Path to file containing the prompt

examples: twelvelabs generate text VIDEO_ID --prompt "Describe this video"
twelvelabs generate text VIDEO_ID --prompt "List all issues" > output.txt
twelvelabs generate text VIDEO_ID -f prompt.txt | jq '.'
text.usage_instructions: Generate free-form text from a video using AI. VIDEO_ID is a required positional argument. Provide the prompt with --prompt/-p or --prompt-file/-f (not both). Use --temperature/-t to control randomness (0.0=deterministic, 1.0=creative). Output goes to stdout for piping.

json.help: Generate JSON from an indexed video using a custom prompt. The prompt should instruct the model to return valid JSON. By default, validates that the output is valid JSON.

options.name: --prompt-file
options.help: Path to file containing the prompt

examples: twelvelabs generate json VIDEO_ID --prompt "Return a JSON array of issues found"
twelvelabs generate json VIDEO_ID -f prompt.txt > review.json
twelvelabs generate json VIDEO_ID -p "Find issues as JSON" -t 0.1
json.usage_instructions: Generate structured JSON from a video using AI. VIDEO_ID is a required positional argument. Provide the prompt with --prompt/-p or --prompt-file/-f. The prompt should instruct the model to return valid JSON. Use --no-validate to skip JSON validation. Use --temperature/-t to control randomness.

generate.usage_instructions: Generate text or structured JSON from indexed videos using AI. Provide a prompt with --prompt/-p or --prompt-file/-f. Output goes to stdout for easy piping. Use text for free-form generation and json for structured output.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `twelvelabs cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `twelvelabs cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

usage_instructions: Use for video AI operations via the TwelveLabs API. Commands follow `twelvelabs <group> <action>` pattern. Workflow: create an index, upload videos to it, then generate text/JSON from the indexed videos. Most list commands support --table/-t, --filter/-f, --limit/-l, and --properties/-p.
